turns.text: I found this leaf with some spots. Can you help me identify what's wrong?
turns.image_description: Leaf with brown spots on the underside, high contrast lighting, no visible mold

turns.text: The brown spots on the underside could indicate a fungal infection. Would you mind sharing a closer look at the upper surface?
Here's a closer shot of the same leaf.
turns.image_description: Macro shot of the same leaf showing tiny white specks on the upper surface

turns.text: The white specks on the upper surface suggest a different pathogen than typical fungal infections. Let me see if there's more to the leaf structure.
I took a cross-section of the leaf. The tissue around the spots looks damaged.
turns.image_description: Cross-section of the leaf showing necrotic tissue around the spots

turns.text: The necrotic tissue points to a bacterial infection rather than fungal. Let me confirm this with a lab test.

turns.image_description: Lab test slide of the leaf tissue showing Gram-positive bacteria colonies

turns.text: The presence of Gram-positive bacteria confirms a bacterial infection. Finally, can you share any environmental conditions around the plant?
The area has high humidity and we applied pesticide recently.
turns.image_description: Environmental data showing high humidity and recent pesticide application

turns.text:
What disease is affecting the plant based on the evidence?

A) Bacterial blight
B) Fungal leaf spot
C) Powdery mildew
D) Leaf rust

ground_truth.reasoning_chain: 1. Initial brown spots on underside led to fungal assumption (img_001). 2. White specks on upper surface (img_002) suggested a different pathogen. 3. Necrotic tissue in cross-section (img_003) pointed to bacterial infection. 4. Lab test confirmed Gram-positive bacteria (img_004). 5. High humidity and pesticide use (img_005) confirmed bacterial blight as the cause.
ground_truth.key_difficulty: Initial visual cues are misleading; sequential evidence from multiple angles and tests is required for accurate diagnosis.
